narrative:
## State of the Story: U.S.-Israel Military Operation Against Iran

### Overview
The United States and Israel launched a major military operation against Iran on Saturday, March 1, 2026, which has now entered its third day [1]. President Trump has estimated the conflict could last "about four weeks" and stated the U.S. is "a little ahead of schedule" [1]. The operation, designated "Operation Epic Fury," has resulted in significant casualties on multiple sides [2][3].

### U.S. Military Casualties
Six U.S. service members have been confirmed killed in action [2]. The casualties were announced in stages: three deaths were initially confirmed on Sunday [3], with two additional deaths confirmed Monday when U.S. forces "recovered the remains of two service members from a facility struck during Iran's initial attacks" [2]. Four additional U.S. service members have been injured [1], while another report indicates five are "seriously wounded" with others sustaining "minor shrapnel injuries and concussions" [3].

In a separate incident, three U.S. fighter jets were "mistakenly shot down by Kuwaiti air defenses Sunday night," though all six aircrew "ejected safely and are in stable condition" [2].

### Iranian Leadership Eliminated
The operation has successfully eliminated key Iranian leadership. Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was killed "on the war's first day" with "targeting assistance from the US" provided to Israel [1]. Other high-ranking officials killed include Ali Shamkhani (secretary of Iran's Defense Council), Mohammed Pakpour (commander of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps), Defense Minister Amir Nasirzadeh, and Abdolrahim Mousavi (chief of staff of Iran's armed forces) [3]. Israel reported eliminating "a majority of the highest-ranking senior military officials of the Iranian security leadership" [3].

### Military Objectives and Damage
The U.S. has "accomplished some of its military objectives, including sinking part of Iran's navy and striking Iranian ballistic missile and nuclear facilities" [1]. The operation has also damaged "multiple Tehran hospitals" though "there are no known deaths associated with those strikes" [1].

### Civilian Casualties
The conflict has produced substantial civilian casualties, particularly in Iran. The most severe single incident occurred when "a missile struck a girls' elementary school in Minab, Iran" shortly after the war began, "reportedly killing at least 175 people, many of them children" [1]. The school was in session due to Iran's Saturday workweek, though "classes had been canceled shortly before the deadly strike, but students had not yet had time to evacuate" [1].

Civilian death toll estimates vary significantly between sources. Iran's Red Crescent reports "at least 555 people have been killed in Iran since the war began" [1], while the Human Rights Activist News Agency (HRANA) provides higher figures, reporting "at least 742 civilians have now been killed in Iran, with nearly 1,000 injured and more than 600 deaths still under review" as of Monday [1].

### Iranian Retaliation
Iran has responded with "missile and drone strikes in retaliation" targeting "Israel and the Gulf states" [1]. Kuwait has been among the targets, with strikes "hitting near Kuwait's international airport and U.S. facilities" [2]. The retaliation has created a "chaotic" air war environment [2].

### Political Context
Public support for the operation appears limited, with a Reuters/Ipsos poll showing "just 1 in 4 Americans supported the U.S. strikes on Iran—with 43 percent saying they disapproved" [3]. This comes as Trump's overall approval rating shows "a majority of Americans (60 percent) expressing disapproval of his presidency" in pre-operation polling [3].

citations.title: The civilians dying in Trump's new war
citations.quote: The US war in Iran, joined by Israel, is in its third day, with no sign of slowing. On Monday, President Donald Trump estimated that it could last about four weeks — 'and we're a little ahead of schedule,' he said.

citations.title: Iran conflict escalates: 6 U.S. service members killed as death toll climbs
citations.quote: Monday afternoon, U.S. Central Command confirmed that U.S. forces recovered the remains of two service members from a facility struck during Iran's initial attacks in the region.

citations.title: Trump Addresses First U.S. Deaths in Iran War, Possibility of Talks
citations.quote: Three U.S. service members have been killed in action and five are seriously wounded as part of Operation Epic Fury

keyDevelopments: U.S.-Israel military operation against Iran began Saturday, March 1, 2026
Six U.S. service members confirmed killed, with additional wounded
Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and other top officials eliminated
Civilian school strike in Minab, Iran kills approximately 175, mostly children
Three U.S. fighter jets shot down by friendly fire from Kuwaiti forces
Iran retaliating with missile and drone strikes on Gulf states
Civilian death toll in Iran ranges from 555 to over 740 depending on source
